agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through lifecycle states (trial, active, cancelled)."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect subscription fees; has a validity/status (valid, failed)."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring time period for which a subscriber is charged (e.g., monthly, annual)."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated process of charging the subscriber's payment method to extend the subscription for the next billing cycle."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to collect payment during the renewal window. Up to 3 attempts are made for standard tiers."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A time window granted to Premium tier subscribers (14 days) during which the subscription remains active despite failed payments, replacing the 3-strike rule."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A zero-cost subscription period offered to new customers, limited to one active trial per email address."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription tier that receives preferential treatment during renewal failure (14-day grace period instead of 3-strike cancellation)."},{"term":"Cancelled Subscription","definition":"A terminal subscription state from which reactivation is impossible; the customer must create a new subscription."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription with a known-bad payment method would put the system into an inconsistent state — an active subscription that can never be billed. This protects data consistency: an active subscription MUST have a viable path to collect revenue. No VP policy change should allow the system to create unbillable active subscriptions.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When activateSubscription() is called, IF the associated payment method status is 'failed', THEN the activation MUST be rejected and the subscription MUST NOT transition to 'active' state."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy decision balancing retry opportunity against customer experience. A VP could reasonably change this to 5 days or 10 days based on payment processor SLAs, retry success data, or customer feedback. The system would not be corrupt if this window changed — it is an operational tuning parameter.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: The renewal process MUST initiate exactly 7 days before the billing cycle end date for each active subscription."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The threshold of 3 attempts is a business policy governing dunning strategy. This number could be changed to 2 or 5 based on churn analysis or payment processor guidance without corrupting system state. It is a configurable policy, not a physical or logical constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a standard-tier subscription accumulates 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts within a billing cycle, the system MUST transition the subscription to 'cancelled' state automatically."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. 'Cancelled' is a terminal state. Allowing transitions out of a terminal state would violate the subscription lifecycle model and create ambiguity in billing history, audit trails, and revenue recognition. If a cancelled subscription could be reactivated, the system would lose the ability to reason about subscription identity — which billing cycle applies? What was the cancellation date? The new-subscription requirement preserves clean audit trails and state integrity.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Given a subscription in 'cancelled' state, any attempt to transition it to 'active' state MUST be rejected. The only path to an active subscription for that customer is creating a new subscription entity."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day grace period and its application specifically to Premium tier are business policy decisions driven by customer value segmentation. The duration could be adjusted (7 days, 30 days) and the eligible tiers could change based on business strategy. The system would not be corrupt if these parameters changed — this is a configurable retention policy.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a Premium tier subscription has a failed renewal, the system MUST NOT cancel it until 14 calendar days have elapsed since the first failure. During that 14-day window, the subscription MUST remain active. The 3-strike rule MUST NOT apply to Premium tier subscriptions."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data consistency and anti-abuse constraint. Allowing multiple concurrent trials per email would corrupt the business model — trials exist to convert prospects, not to provide indefinite free service. Violating this constraint means the system cannot enforce its commercial terms. Unlike a policy threshold (e.g., trial duration), uniqueness of an active trial per identity is a structural integrity rule that prevents exploitation and ensures each email maps to at most one trial entity.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When createFreeTrial() is called for an email address, IF an active trial subscription already exists for that email, THEN the creation MUST be rejected and no new trial entity MUST be created."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer with a payment method marked as 'failed'","when":"They attempt to activate a subscription","then":"The system rejects the activation and returns an error indicating the payment method must be updated"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending on date D","when":"The current date reaches D minus 7 days","then":"The system initiates the auto-renewal process for that subscription"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard-tier subscription with 2 prior failed renewal attempts in the current cycle","when":"The 3rd renewal attempt also fails","then":"The system automatically transitions the subscription to 'cancelled' state and notifies the customer"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in 'cancelled' state","when":"Any actor attempts to reactivate it","then":"The system rejects the reactivation and requires creation of a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal attempt","when":"Fewer than 14 days have elapsed since the first failure","then":"The subscription remains active and the 3-strike cancellation rule is not applied"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal","when":"14 days elapse since the first failure without a successful payment","then":"The system automatically cancels the subscription"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An email address that already has an active free trial subscription","when":"A request is made to create another free trial for the same email","then":"The system rejects the request and returns an error indicating only one active trial per email is allowed"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An email address whose previous free trial has ended or been cancelled","when":"A request is made to create a new free trial for that email","then":"The system must determine whether re-trial is permitted (spec does not address this — see escalations)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Payment method fails between validation and activation","expected_behavior":"The system should re-validate the payment method at the moment of activation, not rely on a cached status. If it fails at activation time, reject."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's payment succeeds on day 13 of grace period","expected_behavior":"The grace period ends, the subscription remains active, and the billing cycle resets normally."},{"scenario":"Customer has a cancelled trial and requests a new paid subscription","expected_behavior":"Permitted — the one-trial-per-email rule only governs active trials, not paid subscriptions."},{"scenario":"Multiple renewal attempts succeed after earlier failures (standard tier, attempt 2 of 3)","expected_behavior":"The failure counter resets upon successful payment. The subscription remains active."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber also hits 3 failed attempts within the 14-day grace period","expected_behavior":"The 3-strike rule does NOT apply to Premium. Only the 14-day clock governs cancellation."},{"scenario":"Customer changes email on an account that has an active trial","expected_behavior":"The uniqueness constraint should be evaluated — does the old email free up for a new trial? Does the new email get checked? Spec is silent on this."},{"scenario":"Subscription activation attempted with no payment method on file at all","expected_behavior":"Should be rejected — no payment method is worse than a failed one. The spirit of BR-1 requires a valid payment method."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"These rules directly govern revenue collection. The renewal window (BR-2) and retry logic (BR-3/BR-5) determine involuntary churn rates. The grace period for Premium reduces churn on highest-value customers. Trial uniqueness prevents revenue leakage from trial abuse.","operational":"Auto-renewal 7 days early creates a retry buffer that reduces payment failure-driven support tickets. The terminal cancellation state (BR-4) simplifies billing reconciliation and audit — no ambiguous reactivation states to track.","user":"Customers experience predictable subscription behavior. Premium customers get preferential treatment matching their tier value. The hard cancellation rule may frustrate customers who want to resume, but it trades convenience for billing clarity.","risk":"The cancelled-is-terminal rule (BR-4) is the highest-risk policy from a customer satisfaction standpoint — competitors may allow reactivation. The 3-strike limit could cause involuntary churn for customers with temporary payment issues. The trial uniqueness rule could be circumvented via email aliases (user+tag@domain.com).","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard Tier)","impact":"Subject to 3-strike cancellation; must create new subscription after cancellation."},{"group":"Customers (Premium Tier)","impact":"Receive 14-day grace period providing more time to resolve payment issues."},{"group":"Finance/Revenue Team","impact":"Terminal cancellation state ensures clean revenue recognition. Retry window affects cash flow timing."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle complaints from customers who cannot reactivate cancelled subscriptions. Need clear scripts for guiding customers to create new subscriptions."},{"group":"Anti-Fraud/Abuse Team","impact":"Trial uniqueness rule is a first line of defense but may need strengthening against email alias abuse."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is relatively clear and internally consistent. Six rules are explicitly stated with enough detail to derive testable criteria. Confidence is not higher because several operational details are unspecified (retry timing within the 7-day window, re-trial eligibility, email alias handling). No contradictions detected.","high_confidence_areas":["Subscription activation requires valid payment method (BR-1)","Cancelled is a terminal state (BR-4)","One active trial per email (BR-6)","Premium grace period replaces 3-strike rule (BR-5)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether a customer whose trial ended can start another trial (one active at a time vs. one ever)","How renewal retry attempts are spaced within the 7-day window","Whether Premium subscribers can also receive retry attempts during their 14-day grace period or only time-based grace","Email alias handling for trial uniqueness (user+tag@domain.com)","What happens to subscriptions if a payment method is removed entirely (not failed, but absent)"],"assumptions":["The 3-strike rule counts consecutive failures within a single renewal cycle, not lifetime","Premium grace period starts from the first failed renewal attempt in a cycle","Trial uniqueness is enforced per exact email string (not normalized)","A successful payment during the retry/grace window resets the failure state"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The rule states 'cannot have more than one active trial per email at any time' — this is clear for concurrent trials, but ambiguous about whether a customer whose trial ended or was cancelled may start a second trial. 'At any time' could mean 'at any given moment' (concurrent restriction) or 'ever' (lifetime restriction). This significantly impacts customer acquisition strategy.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Team","Marketing/Growth Team","Customers"],"options":["Interpret as concurrent-only: one active trial at a time, re-trials permitted after previous trial ends","Interpret as lifetime: one trial ever per email address, no re-trials"],"recommendation":"Clarify with Product. Concurrent-only is more customer-friendly and standard in the industry, but lifetime restriction is stronger for abuse prevention. Recommend concurrent-only with a cooldown period."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Premium tier gets a '14-day grace period instead of the 3-strike rule' — it is unclear whether retries still occur during the 14-day grace period or whether the system simply waits 14 days and then cancels. If retries occur, how many and at what frequency?","affected_stakeholders":["Premium Customers","Finance Team","Payment Operations"],"options":["14-day grace with continued retry attempts (e.g., daily or every 3 days)","14-day grace with no retries — single check at day 14","14-day grace as a pure time extension, with the same 3 retries spread across 14 days"],"recommendation":"Retries should continue during the grace period (option A) — the grace period should increase the chance of successful collection, not just delay cancellation. Recommend daily retries during the 14-day window."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The 3-strike rule MUST NOT apply to Premium tier subscriptions."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data consistency and anti-abuse constraint. Allowing multiple concurrent trials per email would corrupt the business model — trials exist to convert prospects, not to provide indefinite free service. Violating this constraint means the system cannot enforce its commercial terms. Unlike a policy threshold (e.g., trial duration), uniqueness of an active trial per identity is a structural integrity rule that prevents exploitation and ensures each email maps to at most one trial entity.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When createFreeTrial() is called for an email address, IF an active trial subscription already exists for that email, THEN the creation MUST be rejected and no new trial entity MUST be created."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer with a payment method marked as 'failed'","when":"They attempt to activate a subscription","then":"The system rejects the activation and returns an error indicating the payment method must be updated"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending on date D","when":"The attempts in the current cycle","when":"The 3rd renewal attempt also fails","then":"The system automatically transitions the subscription to 'cancelled' state and notifies the
